rag_rules: Fix type mismatches by aligning helper declaration types with expression types; use int, real, bool as AGREE scalar types.
Resolve 'Couldn't resolve reference' errors by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Ensure AGREE expressions maintain type consistency: numeric variables must be assigned numeric expressions.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference unresolved identifiers or use AADL properties as AGREE variables.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers exist in the current scope.
Type consistency in AGREE expressions must match the expected data types of variables and operations.
Avoid introducing new package-qualified identifiers unless they are explicitly imported or defined in the model.
Use only visible AADL features or previously declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Helper declarations must specify correct types: `eq name : type = expression;` or `const name : type = value;`.
Guarantees and assumptions should reference only visible features or declared helpers in the same scope.
Assignments in AGREE are written as `assign output = expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not attempt to resolve missing references by inventing new declarations in the AGREE annex.
Do not mix AADL property names directly into AGREE expressions without explicit model support.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
